rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO** IN THE MATTER OF THE BERNARD LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TRANSFER to SAN MATEO County
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/2021 to be heard with the conservatorship petition. Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of the petition. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUEY KIM WONG LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/2021. Issues: 1) See LRSF 14.36 I. 2): The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). 2) Please explain on page 5 line 18 of the petition alleging the filing fee is $465.00. The court filing fee was $435.00. 3) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LILIA M. MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Whether $63,680.00 in attorney fees and $4,568.00 in costs is reasonable. Attorneys request the fees be placed as a lien against conservatee's real property with 5% interest compounded annually and applied retroactively to the date of the order. Luis Martinez has filed declarations objecting. 2) Attorney requests additional fees of $4,205.00 for anticipated additional services to close the conservatorship. 3) Whether $245.00 in overdraft fees at Wells Fargo should be paid back to the estate. 4) Conservatorship incurred late fees/interest of $286.79 on American express bills. Whether $286.79 in late fees/interest on American Express bills should be paid back to the estate.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KLARA HOLZER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Attachment 6 was only one declination from the Swiss Benevolent Society. The proof of service did not show that notice was given to the DHCS, as alleged in paragraph 30 of the petition. Why were there receipts of dividends when assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period consisted of just cash, an irrevocable burial [trust], and tangible personal property that was apparently never moved into a storage unit and just kept in the conservatee's former apartment at $900/month, although she moved to Silverado in June 2019? Why was there a refund of an overcharged fee for Reverse Mortgage Solutions on 6/26/20? The names of the trust beneficiaries was not set forth in paragraph 32 of the petition. [LRSF 14.48 I.5.c.] (Why was there time spent to "investigate referral" during a second accounting period?

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OMAR ABDULLAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/2021 1) Please explain why the attorney requests reimbursement of a $540.00 filing fee for General Conservatorship Petition on page 6 line 1 of the petition when the filing fee paid was $450.00. 2) Please file conservator timesheets that conform to LRSF 14.53. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. 3) Please explain and provide legal basis for why the conservator is requesting fees for tolls and mileage when LRSF 14.58 says tolls and mileage are absobed as part of fees. It appears this needs to be amended to remove the request for tolls and mileage. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER SOO HOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/21. (No earlier availability.) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Merely stating the total current (not even the true "appraised") value of "Cash, Securities, Mutual Funds" and dividing that value by the number of residuary beneficiaries is insufficient. The amended petition does not disclose what actions the executor took pursuant to his IAEA authority [CRC 7.550(b)], although the summary of the T. Rowe Price statement seems to indicate that the shares of AT&T and Walgreens Boots Alliance stock were sold. What happened to the tangible personal property? (The transfer of cash from one account into another is not a change in the form of assets.) If the general pecuniary bequests have not yet been made, they must be made with interest now as more than a year has passed since the date of death. [Prob. C. Sect. 12003.] The executor never petitioned to increase bond even after the additional savings account was discovered. [CRC 7.204(a).] Paragraph 15 states that a federal estate tax return will be filed... but the return has not been audited... (?) The proof of service filed on 6/30/21 shows the date of mailing as 6/30/31. (Letters Testamentary, not of Special Administration, were issued on 7/29/2020. Rev. & Tax. Code Sect. 19513 was repealed in 2018.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALDSON LEO POWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/21. Still no courtesy copy of the petition has been received by the assigned Examiner. Courtesy copies are file stamped hard copies and must be provided at least five (5) Court days before a hearing. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] The proposed order indicates that a portion of the estate will be distributed to an estate. If an heir post-deceased the decedent, the successor-in-interest to that heir or beneficiary must be clearly identified and proof of that person's standing must be filed. [LRSF 14.36 G.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSE MARY WILLIAMS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust Restatement, Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee; And Complaint For Elder Financial Abuse And Tenant Harassment
rulings.Rulings: continued to 9/13/21. the POS of the NOH filed 6/28/21 is not proof of proper service, as the hearing date on the notice is 7/12/21, and notice was mailed 6/26/21, which is not 30 days before the hearing date on the notice. in addition, the notice did not identify the pleading as an AMENDED petition, and there is still no proof of direct service on Yulanda. service on the attorney is in addition to direct notice required. Finally, the venue section in the amended petition does not sufficiently address the judge's concerns, as it does not discuss the principal place of administration of the trust. a supplement must be filed addressing that particular aspect of the venue issue. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARION JENKINS AND GANAIL JENKINS LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Instructing Trustee To Place Property In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if this is the proper venue, when the successor trustee has reconfirmed that he resides in Oakland. The proof of service filed on 6/21/21 shows that a copy of only the third supplement was served with the Notice of Hearing on 6/21/21 for this hearing. Copies of the first and second supplements were never timely mailed: the proof of service filed on 5/24/21 indicated that copies of the first and second supplements were also included with that correct form of notice, but that notice was mailed on 5/24/21 for a hearing on 6/14/21 -- so, not 30 days before that hearing. [CRC 7.53(b).] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEUNG KOOT TSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete at item 2 (address and telephone). The proof of service is also the same one that had not been completely verified before, so the proof of service was obviously not correctly signed and verified now by the person who did the mailing then (including with the corrected spelling of the niece's name). When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who was (or is) the parent of niece Danika Tse? If her parent was a predeceased sibling, that person must be identified and his/her death stated. Courtesy copies of the completed Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities and corrected proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/20/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STUART SELIGSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/16/2021 court calendar full. Issues: 1) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Court to determine at hearing whether the declaration of due diligence is sufficient or whether more needs to be done. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/2/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
